doctrine.aliases: version-unpinned. sonnet/opus/fable/haiku are platform aliases; the platform resolves each to the provider-recommended version, and resolution can legitimately differ by provider, ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_*_MODEL, CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL, availableModels allowlists, org restrictions, and modelOverrides. Kiln never resolves a Claude alias and never claims which concrete version will serve; it records the RESOLVED identity per run from the modelUsage keys of the archived stream-json result events. gpt-sol names the codex transport model via the resolver below — codex -m requires a concrete id, so the one versioned string in the product lives there and nowhere else.
doctrine.degradation: no silent model substitution and no fallback rung exists: a broken or unavailable codex transport is the exit-20 honest halt (the crash-broken-codex ruling). Any future fallback enters as a fully-specified doctrine change — trigger class, attempt count, actual-model record, receipt location — never as a bare resolver entry.
doctrine.xhigh: permitted only for extremely-complex, all-layers, global-vision work, selected deliberately per artifact — never required, never a standing default (INTAKE-14b; CONSTITUTION.md:66 'xhigh only by deliberate selection; never ladder tiers on one artifact').
doctrine.seats: INTAKE-19 seat philosophy: the thinking seats — the big decisions and slice creation, stage-law — run Fable 5, the strongest mind (opus in succession); the donkey work is cost-optimized — GPT-5.6 as the main coder for logic and mixed, Opus implementing ui and creative pieces perfectly from a well-defined Fable plan.

driver.note: the operator's session model — Kiln never overrides it

kernel-leg.note: hands/idsFetch: model-backed, so HIGH (Q1 ruling: a model-backed role defaults HIGH; 'mechanical' means NO model). The truly-mechanical alternative — replacing these legs with deterministic harness execution, no model call at all — is a named SHIP-era option, not built in this slice.

stage-card.note: validate/report card agents and the claude gate leg — the `act` default for every stage but LAW (INTAKE-19: the LAW stage resolves through stage-law). The build SLICE leg (build + repair) is NOT stage-card: the kernel surface-routes it to builder-ui/builder-logic before agent() (T-03', §2.3). RAISED medium -> high per INTAKE-14 default (kernel.js:188 was the flagged gap)

stage-law.note: the thinking seat — slice creation and the law (INTAKE-19). Fable preferred: the platform resolves the fable alias to the strongest Fable-class mind; opus is the recorded succession when a platform lacks fable — flip the alias here, one file, one line.

builder-ui.note: CONSUMED via surface_routing at the KERNEL's builder-leg dispatch (T-03'): the kernel selects the role from the slice's surface BEFORE agent(), never the already-dispatched card. Live routing data, not a reserved key. Opus implements the interfaces and creative pieces from the locked plan (INTAKE-19); fable is reserved for the thinking seats.

builder-logic.note: ROUTED (simple-fire, supersedes the 2026-07-19 park): the seat is a claude context-builder whose CODER is GPT-5.6 via ONE bash codex exec call per cards/build.md and references/codex-prompt-guide.md — the resolver names the concrete id (gpt-sol); coder effort high (named once here — build.md and the guide cite this note, never a hardcoded value). The claude-family route-target boot rule now holds BY DESIGN: the agent() spawner sees only this sonnet wrapper; codex rides a bash call with stdin closed by the prompt redirect. Cross-family law downstream: GPT coded the slice, so its gate is a fresh claude reviewer in the kernel, never codex. Wrapper effort RAISED medium -> high per the INTAKE-14b/Q1 HIGH-default law (a model-backed role defaults HIGH — the medium was unruled drift, now cured); the coder effort above is separate and stays high.

reviewer-gate.note: the cross-family terminal gate (scripts/kiln-review transport) for ui slices — a logic or mixed slice is GPT-coded, so its gate is a fresh claude mind in the kernel (cross-family law). INTAKE-14 default HIGH. REMIT: after Opus builds the ui, GPT reviews correctness against the locked criteria ONLY — creative direction, visual taste, and aesthetic choices are the Opus builder's and are out of the reviewer's remit, never a finding (v3 duo-pool ui pool, retired/v3/data/duo-pool.json:4 — la-peintresse builds, the-curator reviews static correctness). The prompt enforces it in scripts/kiln-review.

fallback-reviewer.note: the codex-absent fallback reviewer (the v3 duo-pool logic_fallback restored — retired/v3/data/duo-pool.json:7: sonnet builds, OPUS reviews cross-context, the best split without a second family). Consumed on the degraded path only: when a logic or mixed slice was built single-family because codex was down, the kernel convenes a fresh opus mind — a different, stronger model than the sonnet builder, same family and fresh context — for the SAME full validation the normal claude gate runs; the seal still reads the .kiln/degraded marker at seal time and stays single-family. A boot-required consumer key (kernel.js TIER_ROLES): the kernel resolves it through tier() at the degraded gate, so the boot gate now demands it and fails closed with the named configuration fact when it is absent — never a later throw when codex dies mid-run.

brainstorm-facilitator.note: Da Vinci — inherits the session model (agents/da-vinci.md has no model key; stays that way; documented default is inherit per the sub-agents contract)

haiku-migration.note: STRICTLY the explicit haiku-class migration seat (INTAKE-14c: haiku -> sonnet, medium). Never a justification for LOW on any model-backed role; a truly mechanical job takes no model, not a cheap one. No rework consumer today — the landing pad for any surviving haiku-class seat.

dev-sol.note: dev-process Sol reviews (scripts/dev/sol-review.sh defaults already match); xhigh deliberate-only per HANDOFF.md:56
